fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Connecticut Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Connecticut - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity connecticut, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to connecticut, pharmacy technician compact connecticut, connecticut pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Connecticut Commission of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address:
Connecticut Department of Public Health
410 Capitol Avenue, MS #12HSP
PO Box 340308
Hartford, CT 06134-0308

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Connecticut.

steps.title: Review Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you meet the eligibility criteria set by the Connecticut Department of Public Health. Applicants must be at least 16 years of age and satisfy one of the following: be enrolled in a high school or college; be a high school graduate or holder of a general equivalency diploma (GED); or be certified by a national certification body approved by the CT Department of Consumer Protection.

steps.title: Create an eLicense Connecticut Account
steps.description: Navigate to the eLicense Connecticut portal at https://elicense.ct.gov/. If you are a first-time user, click on 'Register a New User Account' to set up your profile. If you have an existing account, log in with your credentials.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application Form
steps.description: Once logged into your eLicense CT account, select the option to 'Apply for a New License'. Search for or select 'Pharmacy Technician' from the list of available professions. Accurately complete all sections of the online application form as prompted.

steps.title: Upload Required Supporting Documents
steps.description: Electronically submit all necessary supporting documentation through the eLicense portal. This typically includes a photocopy of official verification of your high school diploma or GED, an official college transcript, or official verification of enrollment in high school/college, or documentation from a national certification body, depending on your eligibility pathway.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $50.00 directly through the eLicense Connecticut portal. Payment can usually be made via credit/debit card or electronic check.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the PTCB. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Connecticut pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Connecticut have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Connecticut. To work in Connecticut, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Connecticut?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Connecticut while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Connecticut
hero.dek: Apply online for a Pharmacy Technician license through Connecticut's official eLicense portal (elicense.ct.gov). First-time users must create an account, then locate and complete the Pharmacy Technician application, uploading all required documents and paying the fee.

official_links.registration_steps: First-time users must create a new account on the eLicense Connecticut portal by clicking 'Register a New User Account'. After successful registration, log in to access the application forms.
